readme.purpose: Annual take-home withdrawal targets — the cash you receive, post-tax. Taxes (federal, state, NIIT, excise) are computed separately and debited from brokerage on top of this amount. Enter what you want to spend, not a grossed-up number. Actual withdrawals may be scaled down in bad markets per economicglobal.json withdrawal shock settings.
readme.floor_k: Minimum take-home in $k regardless of market conditions. Simulator will not scale below this even in severe downturns. Taxes are still debited from brokerage separately even at the floor.

schedule.years: Year range e.g. '1-5'. Simulation year 1 = current_age + 1.
schedule.amount_k: Your target take-home in $k — the cash deposited to your bank account, in today's purchasing power (inflation-adjusted annually). Taxes are paid separately from brokerage and are NOT deducted from this amount. Do not gross up for taxes. Market scaling may reduce it in severe downturns.
schedule.base_k: Minimum acceptable take-home in $k for this period (above floor_k, below amount_k). Models spending flexibility — how much you can cut if needed. Also the inflation base: floor and target both grow from this value each year. Set equal to floor_k to have the floor inflate in step with the target.
